IF NO SUCH AUTHORITIES MAY BE FOUNDED, SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF SHALL STATE SO. BRIEF SHALL NOT INCLUDE ANY CITATIONS TO AUTHORITIES THAT DO NOT INVOLVE PARTITION ACTIONS. JUDGE: CHARLES F. Off Calendar Jan-27-2023 Continued To Apr-21-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO APPOINT DECEASED PLAINTIFFS LONG-TERM SPOUSE CAROLYN WILLIAMS AS HIS SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST IN THIS ACTION is continued to April 21, 2023. Successor-in-interest to provide a copy of the title/probate order, etc. showing that she is the new legal owner of 50% interest in the property by no later than April 14, 2023. Alternatively, successor-in-interest may file a supplemental brief no later than April 14, 2023, not to exceed 2 pages, limited to citations to authority permitting an heir to proceed as a party in a partition action. If no such authorities may be founded, supplemental brief shall state so. Brief shall not include any citations to authorities that do not involve partition actions.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Luke Jackson, Esq. (213.986.4323; luke@voklaw.com) of Vokshori Law Group, APLC for Plaintiff.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Discussion held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF RUSSELL WILLIAMS' Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories From Defendant And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,250.00: Motion is moot. Request for sanctions is GRANTED.

Moving party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to williamclynn@gmail.com

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
